name: GlareDB (Distributed SQL Analytics)
pricing_notes: Open source (AGPL-3.0); GlareDB Cloud available with paid tiers

auth.notes: Local: no auth; GlareDB Cloud: API key

performance.notes: Rust-based vectorized execution; comparable to DuckDB for local; cloud adds network overhead

agent_readiness.notes: SQL analytics engine built in Rust. Query across multiple data sources: Postgres, MySQL, BigQuery, S3 (Parquet, CSV, JSON), Delta Lake, MongoDB, Snowflake. Federated queries join data from different sources in one SQL statement. Python bindings, DataFusion query engine. DuckDB alternative with stronger multi-source federation. Cloud version for collaborative analytics. Early-stage but strong Rust performance foundation.

gotchas: AGPL-3.0 license — commercial use requires attention
best_when: Federated queries across multiple data sources (Postgres + S3 + BigQuery)
Teams wanting DuckDB-like analytics with better multi-source support
Data lakehouse queries without moving data
avoid_when: Single data source (DuckDB is simpler and more mature)
AGPL license restrictions in commercial products
summary: GlareDB is a Rust-based SQL analytics engine for federated queries across Postgres, MySQL, BigQuery, S3, Delta Lake, MongoDB, and Snowflake in a single SQL statement. DataFusion-powered vectorized execution. Python bindings. Alternative to DuckDB with stronger multi-source federation. AGPL-3.0 license. Early-stage but promising for data lakehouse and federated analytics use cases.
